observation.summary: Local QA loaded Figma intent entries but did not expose visual references for humans reviewing the dashboard.

hypothesis.summary: Small PNG references captured from authoritative Figma nodes can make daemon-owned Figma boundary evidence visible without claiming riido-client pixel parity.

execution.summary: Verify golden node identity, dimensions, and SHA-256, then copy verified references into local QA screenshots for dashboard and S3 publication.

evaluation.summary: The verifier fails when the golden PNG hash, dimensions, or node id diverges from the executable manifest.

retrospective.summary: Figma rows now move from hidden visual debt to dashboard-visible screenshot references while the true frontend pixel comparison remains outside daemon ownership.

evidence.proves: Figma golden references are verified and copied into local QA screenshot evidence
Figma screenshot links render in the local QA dashboard
